steps.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, exactly two angles are equal. Angle A = 50°. Three cases to check: (1) A = B, (2) A = C, (3) B = C.

Case 1: A = B = 50°, so C = 180° - 50° - 50° = 80°. Valid triangle. B = 50°. Case 2: A = C = 50°, so B = 180° - 50° - 50° = 80°. Valid. B = 80°. Case 3: B = C, so 50° + 2B = 180°, giving B = 65°. Valid.
Possible values of angle B are 50°, 65°, and 80°. B = 100° is not achievable: if B = 100°, then A + B = 150°, C = 30°. The angles 50°, 100°, 30° have no two equal — not isosceles.

• A: B = 50° — possible (Case 1 above).
  • B: B = 65° — possible (Case 3 above).
  • C: B = 80° — possible (Case 2 above).
  • D: B = 100° — leads to angles 50°, 100°, 30° with no two equal, so not isosceles. That confirms D is correct.
